message:
feat(hook): integrate allowlist checking into hook and CLI pipelines

Completes the allowlist integration by loading and consulting allowlists
in both the main hook entry point and the CLI test command.

src/main.rs (hook mode):
- Load layered allowlists at startup via load_default_allowlists()
- Check allowlists after pack pattern match, before outputting denial
- Silent allow (no output) when allowlist grants exception
- Minimal latency impact: allowlist lookup only on matches

src/cli.rs (dcg test command):
- Load allowlists for parity with hook mode behavior
- Pass allowlists to evaluate_command()
- Display allowlist override information in output:
  "Result: ALLOWED (allowlisted by project)"
  "Allowlist reason: <entry reason>"

This ensures consistent behavior between:
- Hook mode (stdin JSON processing)
- CLI test mode (dcg test "command")

Users can now:
1. Add project allowlist: .dcg/allowlist.toml
2. Add user allowlist: ~/.config/dcg/allowlist.toml
3. Test with: dcg test "docker system prune"
4. See override info when allowlist grants exception

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
